title: Observability: replace eprintln with tracing everywhere
description:
**Problem**
Mixed use of `tracing` and `eprintln!`. For a "daemon for swarms", logs matter.

**Design**
- Use `tracing` everywhere (esp sync start/stop, retries, backoff, durations, remote id)
- Add structured fields for key events
- Consider counters (sync failures, queue depth, refresh latency) - even structured logs help before full prometheus

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No `eprintln!` in non-test code
- [ ] Sync events have structured tracing spans
- [ ] Key metrics visible in logs (durations, retry counts)

**Files:** grep for eprintln, likely src/daemon/*.rs, src/git/*.rs

title: Detect and surface remote force-push/divergence
description:
**Problem**
If the remote history is rewritten (force-push), sync currently treats it like a normal remote update and proceeds. There's no explicit detection or warning to the user.

**Design**
Detect non-ancestor relationships between local/remote refs and surface a structured warning/error (e.g., status payload includes `diverged=true`). Consider requiring manual `bd sync --force` for history rewrites.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Divergence detection is explicit
- [ ] User-visible warning surfaced (status/IPC)
- [ ] Tests cover force-push/divergence scenario

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/api/mod.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Installer hardening: checksum verification + rate-limit robustness
description:
## What's Wrong
Install script fetches `releases/latest` unauthenticated and downloads a tarball without any integrity verification (checksum/signature). It may also fail due to GitHub API rate limits.

## Where
- scripts/install.sh: uses https://api.github.com/.../releases/latest and grep/sed parsing
- scripts/install.sh: downloads release asset and untars with no checksum

## Why It Matters
- Security: users execute a network script piping to bash; we should verify what we download.
- Reliability: rate limiting or JSON format changes can break installs.

## Suggested Fix
- Publish checksums (e.g., SHA256SUMS) as release assets and verify before install.
- Prefer GitHub release page redirect for latest tag, or allow `BD_VERSION` override.
- Use `jq` when available; fallback to robust parsing.

## Acceptance
- Installer verifies integrity of downloaded binary.
- Installer supports explicit version pinning.

title: Release scripts: make version bump portable across macOS/Linux
description:
## What's Wrong
`just release-patch` / `just release-minor` use `sed -i` in a way that's GNU-sed specific; BSD sed (macOS outside nix) requires an extension argument.

## Where
- justfile:67-68, 87-88

## Why It Matters
Contributors running release scripts on macOS without nix will hit a broken release flow.

## Suggested Fix
- Use a portable approach: `perl -pi -e`, or detect BSD vs GNU sed.
- Consider moving release logic to `scripts/release.sh` with explicit checks.

## Acceptance
- Release scripts work on macOS + Linux without requiring GNU sed.

title: IPC: unit ResponsePayload variants serialize as null (ambiguous)
description:
**Problem**
`ResponsePayload` is `#[serde(untagged)]` and includes multiple unit variants (`Synced`, `Initialized`, `Pong`, `ShuttingDown`). These serialize to JSON `null`, so clients can deserialize ambiguously.

Example symptom: `bd init` prints `synced` instead of `initialized`.

**Design**
Switch `ResponsePayload` to an explicit tagged representation (e.g. `#[serde(tag="type", content="data", rename_all="snake_case")]`), or ensure unit variants serialize as distinct strings/objects. Keep backward compatibility via versioning or dual-decode.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd init` prints `initialized` on success
- [ ] Unit ok payloads are not serialized as `null`
- [ ] JSON decoding is deterministic
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/cli/render.rs`
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
complete_sync must not drop local mutations on join failure
description:
**Problem**
In `Daemon::complete_sync`, if mutations happened during sync and `CanonicalState::join` fails, the code discards local state and keeps `synced_state`. That can drop mutations made during the sync window.

**Design**
When join fails, detect/resolution collisions (reuse `git::collision` logic) and retry the merge. If merge still fails, keep local state, mark dirty, and surface/log the failure so operators can intervene.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Local mutations are never dropped on join failure
- [ ] Collision resolution is applied for dirty-after-sync merges
- [ ] Tests cover the join-failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/git/collision.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add kill -9 crash recovery integration test for WAL
description:
Add integration test that:
1. Starts daemon
2. Creates beads
3. SIGKILL daemon (before sync completes)
4. Restarts daemon
5. Verifies beads recovered from WAL

Requires subprocess spawning and signal handling in test harness. See tests/critical_path.rs for existing integration test patterns.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Expose sync retry backoff (next retry time) in daemon/CLI status

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Type-safe daemon: eliminate invariant-checking unwraps
description:
Eliminate unwraps in daemon code by encoding proven invariants in the type system.

**Goal:** No `unwrap()` in non-test daemon paths where failures can be caused by repo state or git IO.

**Approach:** "Parse, dont validate" — move checks to where data enters the system and let types remember what has been validated.

**Subtasks:**
- Serde boundary validation for identity types
- Encapsulate DepKey (no self-loops)
- LoadedRemote proof type
- require_live helper
- with_mutation transaction helper
- Collapse live/tombstones into BeadEntry
- Remove duplicate key from DepEdge

See individual beads for design details.
closed_reason: Core goal achieved: eliminated all .unwrap() calls on bead lookups in daemon paths. Completed: serde boundary validation, DepKey encapsulation, LoadedRemote proof type, require_live helpers + refactoring, with_mutation helper. Remaining low-priority cleanup (bd-mgf, bd-di1) filed as separate beads.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Note ID collisions should be guarded
description:
**Problem**
Note IDs are random 8-char base58; collisions are unlikely but possible. A collision overwrites an existing note in the CRDT map.

**Design**
Include actor + timestamp in note IDs or retry generation until unique in-bead. Add a test that forces collision handling.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Note ID generation is collision-safe
- [ ] Tests cover collision path

**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs

title: Add bd dep cycles detection command
description:
beads-go has 'bd dep cycles' to detect circular dependencies in the dependency graph.

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/dep.rs

title: Remote URL canonicalization + type tightening
description:
**Problem**
Remote URL normalization is too loose (ssh/https variants, ports, users). This can split canonical state across clones and cause data divergence.

**Design**
Add a stricter RemoteUrl parser that:
- strips scheme/user, lowercases host
- normalizes ssh scp-style and ssh:// URLs
- preserves port when present (host:port)
- trims .git and trailing slashes
Add focused tests for common variants (ssh/https/ssh://, with/without user, with port).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Same remote in different URL forms maps to identical RemoteUrl
- [ ] Tests cover common cases + edge cases
- [ ] No regressions for file:// or local paths

**Files:** src/daemon/remote.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Sync fetch must not clobber local ref (ancestry check + backup)
description:
**Problem**
`SyncProcess::fetch` force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to match remote and uses `unwrap_or(Oid::zero())` for ref lookup. If local is ahead/diverged, this overwrites local commits; if ref lookup errors, we treat it as missing. Both can lose durable local history.

**Design**
- Use ref lookup that distinguishes NotFound vs error.
- Only fast-forward local ref when it is an ancestor of remote.
- If local is ahead/diverged, create a backup ref (e.g., `refs/beads/backup/<oid>`) before any update and avoid forcing local to remote.
- Keep commit parent as remote head for linear history.
- Add tests covering local-ahead and diverged scenarios.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No forced local ref update when local ahead/diverged
- [ ] Backup refs created for diverged local history
- [ ] Ref lookup errors are surfaced
- [ ] Tests cover ancestry check + backup behavior
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/git/error.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Migration: make imported comment NoteIds globally unique
description:
## What's Wrong
Go comment notes are imported with `NoteId::new(c.id.to_string())`. If comment IDs are not globally unique across issues in beads-go exports, notes can collide and overwrite/merge unexpectedly.

## Where
- src/migrate/go_export.rs:288 (NoteId::new(c.id.to_string()))

## Why It Matters
Data loss / silent merging during migration: two different comments could end up with the same note ID.

## Suggested Fix
- Make note IDs include the issue ID (and/or created_at) e.g. `go-comment-<issue-id>-<comment-id>`.
- Add a migration test fixture with repeated comment IDs across different issues to verify uniqueness.

## Acceptance
- Import never drops/merges distinct comments due to note ID collisions.
closed_reason: Made imported comment NoteIds globally unique by including issue ID in the format: go-comment-{issue_id}-{comment_id}. Added tests to verify uniqueness across issues.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add --type flag to bd update command
description:
**Problem**
Cannot change a beads type after creation. Had to close bd-d37 and recreate as epic bd-2fr.

**Solution**
Add `--type` flag to `bd update`:
```
bd update <id> --type=epic
```

**Design Notes**
- Should validate type is one of: task, bug, feature, epic, chore
- Type changes should be LWW like other fields
- Consider if changing TO epic should warn if bead has no children
- Consider if changing FROM epic should warn if bead has children

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd update --type <TYPE>` works
- [ ] Invalid types rejected with clear error
- [ ] Type change reflected in `bd show`
darin@book
title: darin@book
Tombstone GC policy can allow ID reuse/resurrection
description:
**Problem**
Tombstones are GC'd after 30 days in sync merge. If an ID is resurrected after GC, collisions can reappear or deleted IDs can be reused unintentionally.

**Design**
Revisit GC: either make tombstones permanent, use a much longer TTL, or store a compact ID-reuse guard (e.g., bloom filter or min hash) to prevent resurrection. Consider making GC configurable.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] GC policy documented and configurable or safe by default
- [ ] Regression test for resurrection after GC

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/core/state.rs, SPEC.md

title: Add command aliases: deps for dep, ls for list
description:
Add common aliases for better discoverability:
- 'deps' and 'dependencies' as aliases for 'dep'
- 'ls' as alias for 'list'

Files: src/cli/mod.rs

title: Smart commit messages from beads context
description:
Make sync commit messages on refs/heads/beads/store more descriptive. Instead of 'sync: +1 created', show what actually changed:

- created bd-xxx: "Title here"
- updated bd-yyy desc: "new description"  
- closed bd-zzz: "reason"
- deleted bd-abc

This helps with debugging and auditing bead changes over time. Since these commits live on a separate ref (beads/store), they won't clutter the main code history.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add bd create --file for bulk creation from markdown
description:
beads-go supports creating beads from a markdown file:
  bd create --file issues.md --type feature

This enables batch creation workflows. Also consider --from-template support.

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/create.rs

title: Daemon init should not ignore ensure_repo_loaded errors; dedupe SyncWait
description:
## What's Wrong
`Request::Init` ignores errors from `ensure_repo_loaded` (TODO in code). Also `SyncWait` behavior is duplicated: `Daemon::handle_request` returns Synced immediately, but `run_state_loop` has special-case barrier logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/core.rs:585 (TODO: ignores ensure_repo_loaded errors after init)
- src/daemon/core.rs:559 (SyncWait returns immediately)
- src/daemon/server.rs:45 (SyncWait handled as a barrier before calling daemon)

## Why It Matters
Init can report success even if repo state isn't loadable, leading to confusing follow-on failures. Duplicated SyncWait logic makes correctness brittle and creates drift.

## Suggested Fix
- Make Init return an error (or include warning details) if `ensure_repo_loaded` fails.
- Consolidate SyncWait into one place (prefer state loop barrier) and remove/clarify the other path.

## Acceptance
- `bd init` fails (or returns structured warning) when it cannot load the repo state.
- `SyncWait` has a single source of truth (no duplicate paths).
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add daemon control commands (stop, restart, status)
description: Add subcommands to 'bd daemon' for controlling the daemon: stop, restart, status. Currently have to kill the process manually.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Git sync state-machine robustness tests
description:
**Problem**
We lack explicit tests for sync behavior under diverged refs, non-fast-forward pushes, and stale local refs. Regressions here can cause data loss or stuck syncs.

**Design**
Add tests that simulate:
- local ahead of remote (backup ref created, local not clobbered)
- remote ahead of local (fast-forward update succeeds)
- local/remote diverged (divergence flagged, sync outcome carries warning)
- push non-fast-forward retry loop respects max_retries
Use temp git repos with controlled commit graphs.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests cover the above scenarios
- [ ] Sync outcome divergence is surfaced
- [ ] No data loss (local commits preserved)

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, tests/git_sync.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Refactor send_request and send_request_no_autostart to share common code
description:
## What's Wrong
send_request and send_request_no_autostart in src/daemon/ipc.rs duplicate the request encoding/response parsing logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/ipc.rs:828-838 (send_request)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs:844-859 (send_request_no_autostart)

## Why It Matters
If we change the wire format or add error handling, we need to update both places.

## Suggested Fix
Extract common send/receive logic into a helper that takes a stream.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Detect force-push and surface explicit warning in sync status

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk

description: darin@book
title: darin@book

description: darin@book
title: darin@book

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Remove just --list from bd prime; remove repo hook in favor of global
description:
Two cleanup items:
1. bd prime currently shows just --list output - remove this
2. The claude code hook in this repo should be removed; leave it configured globally instead

title: Dep updates don't get commit messages
description: When dependencies are updated via bd dep add/remove, the git sync commits don't include meaningful messages about what changed. Should capture the operation in the commit message for traceability.

title: git_worker.rs:78 unwrap can use entry API instead
description: The open() method uses check-then-unwrap pattern that could use entry API for cleaner code. Not a safety issue since we just inserted if not present.

title: Verify bd upgrade downloads with checksums/signatures
description:
**Problem**
Upgrade downloads prebuilt binaries without integrity checks.

**Design**
Publish release checksums (sha256) and validate downloaded archives before install. Prefer signed checksums or sigstore where possible.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Downloaded archives are verified before install
- [ ] Upgrade fails fast on checksum mismatch
- [ ] Tests cover checksum verification

title: Test bead for bv viewer integration

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Harden git sync state machine edge cases
description:
**Problem**
Sync/load/refresh pathways have overlapping but different logic. Edge cases (diverged refs, remote missing ref, local ahead, dirty during refresh, rewritten history) are not fully covered by tests. This risks subtle data loss or confusing behavior.

**Design**
Add a focused sync-state test harness that builds local/remote repos and exercises: missing refs, local-ahead, remote-ahead, diverged, force-push rewrite, fetch failures, dirty during refresh. Assert invariants: no local data loss, backups created, errors surfaced. Consider consolidating merge logic into a single path to reduce divergence.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Tests cover diverged/local-ahead/force-push scenarios.
- [ ] Failures are explicit; no silent state drops.
- [ ] Backups created when local history would be overwritten.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/git_worker.rs, tests/critical_path.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add richer list filtering (date ranges, pattern matching)
description:
beads-go has comprehensive list filtering that beads-rs lacks:
- Date range filters: --created-after, --updated-after, --closed-after
- Pattern matching: --title-contains, --desc-contains
- Priority ranges: --priority-min, --priority-max (partially done)
- Empty/null checks: --no-assignee, --no-labels, --empty-description

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/list.rs

title: Clock time-source injection + monotonic tests
description:
**Problem**
Clock behavior is hard to test deterministically. We should be able to inject time sources to simulate skew and verify monotonicity across restarts.

**Design**
Introduce a TimeSource trait for Clock (SystemTimeSource default). Allow tests to inject a fake source. Add tests covering backward/forward jumps and ensure stamps are monotonic and skew is detected.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Clock supports injected time source in tests
- [ ] Monotonicity tests cover backward/forward jumps
- [ ] No API changes to production callers (Clock::new remains default)

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Nix flake skips tests (doCheck=false) - CI gap
description:
## What's Wrong
flake.nix has doCheck=false to avoid test failures during nix build. This means `nix build` won't catch test regressions.

## Where
- flake.nix:42-43

## Why It Matters
Users installing via nix won't get test validation. If tests break, nix users get broken builds.

## Options
1. Fix whatever causes tests to fail in nix sandbox (likely git/network related)
2. Add a separate `nix flake check` that runs tests outside the build
3. Accept the gap and rely on CI (current state)
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Boundary ID/slug type tightening
description:
**Problem**
Some boundary layers still accept raw strings for IDs/slugs, risking invalid values slipping through.

**Design**
Audit CLI/IPC entrypoints and replace raw string handling with strong types (BeadId/BeadSlug/ActorId) as early as possible. Add validation helpers for common parse paths and remove fallible unwraps.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Parsing/validation centralized
- [ ] No new unwraps
- [ ] Tests cover invalid inputs

**Files:** src/cli/*, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/core/identity.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Blocking freshness loads stall entire daemon
description:
**Problem**
`ensure_repo_fresh()` does a blocking `GitOp::Load` roundtrip on the state thread. Any slow fetch = every client request pauses. OK-ish for small repos, but a design footgun.

**Design**
Make refresh async:
- If repo is clean and refresh TTL hit, kick off background load
- Serve current cached state meanwhile
- Apply refreshed state when it arrives (maybe invalidate caches)
- Optionally: let queries request `fresh=true` when they really need it

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Slow git fetch doesnt block unrelated client requests
- [ ] Stale reads are bounded (TTL still respected eventually)
- [ ] Fresh flag available for queries that need it

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/query_executor.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
CRDT join property tests (commutative/associative/idempotent)
description:
**Problem**
We don’t have property tests proving CRDT join algebra and invariants hold across random states. A subtle regression could cause silent data loss.

**Design**
Add proptest generators for CanonicalState (beads + tombstones + deps) and assert:
- join(a,b) == join(b,a)
- join(a, join(b,c)) == join(join(a,b), c)
- join(a,a) == a
- invariants hold after join (live ∩ tombstones = ∅, dep indexes consistent)
Use minimal valid field generation (safe ActorId/BeadId) and keep sizes small for speed.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Property tests added and deterministic
- [ ] No panics; invariants checked
- [ ] Tests run in CI

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/core/dep.rs, Cargo.toml
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Sync fails when migrating from beads-go in PR branch with existing store
description:
**Problem**
When migrating from beads-go to beads-rs in a PR branch, the sync doesn't work properly. Beads appear not to exist after migration.

**Root Cause**
The `migrate from-go` command bypasses the daemon entirely - it directly writes to `refs/heads/beads/store` using `SyncProcess` (src/cli/commands/migrate.rs:71-74). But the daemon caches state in memory per-remote in `RepoState`. After migration completes:
1. Git ref has the migrated state
2. Daemon still has empty/old cached state
3. CLI queries go through daemon, return stale data

**Design**
Add a `Request::Refresh` IPC command that tells the daemon to reload state from the git ref for a given repo. The migrate command should call this after successful migration.

Alternatively, migrate could go through the daemon IPC entirely, but that's more invasive.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] After `bd migrate from-go`, `bd list` shows the migrated beads
- [ ] Works even if daemon was already running before migration
- [ ] Test: migrate in a fresh clone, verify state is visible

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/commands/migrate.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
closed_reason: Fixed: added Request::Refresh and notify daemon after migration

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Write migration guide for beads-go to beads-rs
description:
Document the migration path for users of the Go version to switch to Rust. Cover:

- Data format changes (if any)
- CLI differences
- New features in beads-rs
- How to migrate existing beads data

This helps adoption and shows the project is mature enough to replace the Go version.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
WAL write failure must abort mutation + preserve state
description:
**Problem**
Mutations can succeed even when WAL writes fail. In `src/daemon/core.rs` `mark_dirty_and_schedule` logs a warning and proceeds, so a crash can lose acknowledged data. There is no rollback of in-memory state when WAL writes fail.

**Design**
Introduce a transactional mutation path that applies changes to a cloned `CanonicalState`, writes the WAL entry first, and only commits the new state + dirty flag on success. Add a dedicated OpError (e.g., `WalError`/`DurabilityError`) surfaced via IPC. Ensure WAL sequence only advances on successful write. Add a unit test that forces WAL write failure and verifies no state change + error return.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL write failures return an error and do not modify repo state
- [ ] WAL sequence/dirty flags remain unchanged on WAL failure
- [ ] Successful WAL writes still schedule sync
- [ ] Tests cover WAL failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/ops.rs, src/daemon/wal.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Persist root slug in meta.json; default new IDs to repo-name
description:
## What's Wrong
Bead IDs now support `<slug>-<suffix>` to match beads-go (repo-name slug), but new repos still default to `bd` because slug selection is inferred from existing state.

## Where
- src/daemon/executor.rs: preferred_bead_slug(...) falls back to "bd" when store is empty
- src/git/wire.rs: meta.json only stores format_version; no place to persist root slug

## Why It Matters
- Fresh repos should default to the repo-name slug (beads-go parity) or at least allow configuring it.
- Inferring slug from existing IDs fails for empty stores and is an implicit heuristic.

## Suggested Fix
- Add `root_slug` to `meta.json` (WireMeta + core Meta), and set it on init (derive from repo dir name or remote URL basename; sanitize).
- Use persisted `root_slug` for new ID generation.
- Decide compatibility strategy (format_version bump if needed; ensure old readers ignore unknown fields or gate by version).

## Acceptance
- After `bd init` in a repo named `foo`, new IDs default to `foo-...` (or a clearly documented rule).
- `bd create` in an empty store uses `meta.root_slug`, not a heuristic.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Crash recovery integration test (kill -9 WAL replay)
description:
**Problem**
We still lack an integration test that proves WAL recovery works after an unclean daemon crash. Data loss here would be catastrophic.

**Design**
Add an integration test that:
1) creates a repo with a bare remote
2) starts the daemon (via bd create --json) and captures PID via IPC Ping
3) performs a mutation (bd create) and verifies WAL file exists
4) SIGKILL the daemon immediately (kill -9) before sync finishes
5) restart via any bd command (autostart)
6) assert the bead is present (bd list/show) and can be synced to remote
Use per-test XDG_RUNTIME_DIR + BD_WAL_DIR temp dirs to avoid cross-test leakage.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Test passes reliably
- [ ] WAL replay is asserted (data present after restart)
- [ ] No flakiness in CI

**Files:** tests/critical_path.rs (or new tests/robustness.rs), src/daemon/ipc.rs (if needed)
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add sync soak harness script (force-push, crash recovery, multi-daemon)

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Refactor query_executor.rs to use require_live helpers
description:
query_executor.rs has instances of the check-then-unwrap pattern that should use the new require_live helpers added in bd-ieo.

Files: src/daemon/query_executor.rs
closed_reason: Refactored query_executor.rs to use require_live with MapLiveError trait for query_show, query_dep_tree, and query_deps.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Move more CLI validation into core types (parse helpers)

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Replace eprintln! usage with tracing and adjust default log levels

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Unify run.rs and server.rs client handling
description:
**Problem**
`run.rs` and `server.rs` both implement near-identical client handling. Duplication.

Also: `handle_request` has a `SyncWait` branch thats basically dead because the state loop intercepts it.

**Design**
- Extract shared client handling into single module
- Prune dead `SyncWait` branch (or make it the single source of truth)
- Reduce overall code duplication

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Single client handling implementation
- [ ] Dead code removed
- [ ] Existing behavior preserved

**Files:** src/daemon/run.rs, src/daemon/server.rs

title: Daemon: remove unwraps in query/executor; enforce invariants via types
closed_reason: Superseded by epic bd-2fr - broken into individual beads

description: darin@book
title: darin@dusk
Collapse live/tombstones into single BeadEntry map
description:
**Problem**
The invariant "live ∩ tombstones = ∅" is enforced by careful method design:
```rust
pub struct CanonicalState {
    live: BTreeMap<BeadId, Bead>,
    tombstones: BTreeMap<BeadId, Tombstone>,
    // ...
}
```

This relies on `insert`/`delete` being correct. A bug could violate the invariant.

**Design**
Make the invariant structurally impossible:
```rust
pub enum BeadEntry {
    Live(Bead),
    Tombstone(Tombstone),
}

pub struct CanonicalState {
    beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>,
    deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>,
}
```

Now a bead cannot be both live and tombstoned - the type system prevents it.

**Design Notes**
- Bigger refactor than the other type improvements
- Consider doing this when touching `state.rs` for other reasons
- `get_live`/`get_tombstone` become pattern matches on entry
- Iteration patterns change (`live.values()` → `beads.values().filter_map(...)`)
- Wire format (`src/git/wire.rs`) may need adjustment

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `BeadEntry` enum in `src/core/state.rs`
- [ ] `CanonicalState` uses single `beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>`
- [ ] All accessors (`get_live`, `get_tombstone`, iteration) updated
- [ ] Wire format handles the change
- [ ] Invariant comment updated to note its now structural
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/git/wire.rs

title: Durability gap: ops succeed before anything hits disk
description:
**Problem**
Mutations update in-memory state and schedule a sync later. If the process crashes or power dies before the debounce fires, acknowledged work is lost.

**Design Options**

*Option A:* Local WAL/journal
Append-only ops or periodic snapshot in the socket dir; replay on daemon start.

*Option B:* Immediate local-only git commit  
On each mutation, write a local-only beads commit immediately (durable), push/compact later. Adds commits but can squash in compaction pass.

**Design Notes**
User concern: Option B adds commits. Consider squash-on-push or a separate local ref that gets force-pushed after compaction.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Crash after acknowledged mutation doesnt lose data
- [ ] Recovery mechanism tested (kill -9 during burst, restart, verify state)
- [ ] Performance acceptable (write latency)

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
closed_reason: Implemented WAL-based durability. Replaced local git commits with atomic WAL file writes (~10x faster). Recovery on startup merges WAL with git state. Unit tests pass; full kill -9 integration test deferred.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
bd ready: sort by priority (P0 first, P4 last)
description: Currently bd ready shows issues in some order but should be sorted by priority so that P0 issues appear first and P4 issues appear last. This helps agents pick the most important work first.

title: Clock skew can break LWW ordering
description:
**Problem**
HLC relies on wall clock time. If a machine clock jumps backward or is far ahead, its stamps can dominate LWW merges and effectively overwrite other actors' updates. This can look like data loss.

**Design**
Persist the last-seen WriteStamp (per-repo) and clamp local wall_ms to >= last_seen.wall_ms. If local time moves backwards, advance logical counter instead of wall time. Add skew detection (warn if now << last_seen or now >> last_seen by threshold). Consider storing max observed stamp in meta or WAL to survive restarts.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Stamps remain monotonic across restarts and backward clock jumps.
- [ ] Skew detection is surfaced (status/log).
- [ ] Tests simulate backward/forward jumps and verify monotonicity + merge ordering.

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Disable tombstone GC to prevent resurrection/data loss edge cases

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Wire format roundtrip + fuzz tests
description:
**Problem**
Wire serialization/parsing lacks property-level coverage. Corrupted or unexpected data could silently lose fields.

**Design**
Add proptest-based roundtrip tests:
- serialize_state/parse_state
- serialize_tombstones/parse_tombstones
- serialize_deps/parse_deps
- serialize_meta/parse_meta (including last_write_stamp optional)
Additionally test tolerant parsing of legacy/extra fields (if applicable).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Roundtrip property tests added
- [ ] Meta last_write_stamp preserved
- [ ] Tests run fast in CI

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs, Cargo.toml
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add derived indexes to avoid full dep scans
description:
Many queries do repeated full scans of deps (`deps_from`, `compute_blocked_by`, cycle detection, validation). Fine at 200 issues, painful at 20k.

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/daemon/query_executor.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
design:
**In-memory only** — indexes live in `RepoState`, never persisted to deps.jsonl.

**Rebuild on load:** When daemon starts or merges from git, iterate deps once to build:
```rust
struct DepIndexes {
    out_edges: BTreeMap<BeadId, Vec<(BeadId, DepKind)>>,  // from -> [(to, kind)]
    in_edges: BTreeMap<BeadId, Vec<(BeadId, DepKind)>>,   // to -> [(from, kind)]
}
```

**Incremental update:** In `apply_add_dep` / `apply_remove_dep`, push/remove from both maps.

**Functions to change:**
- `deps_from()` (state.rs:300) → lookup `out_edges[id]`
- `deps_to()` (state.rs:309) → lookup `in_edges[id]`  
- `compute_blocked_by()` (query_executor.rs:707) → iterate `in_edges` filtered by kind
- `would_create_cycle()` (executor.rs:1073) → BFS uses `out_edges` directly

Complexity drops from O(all deps) to O(neighbors per hop).

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Add require_live helper to eliminate bead lookup boilerplate

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Encapsulate DepKey to enforce no self-loops by construction

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Remove duplicate key from DepEdge
description:
**Problem**
`DepKey` is stored both as the map key AND inside `DepEdge.key`:
```rust
deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>

pub struct DepEdge {
    pub key: DepKey,      // <-- duplicates the map key
    pub created: Stamp,
    pub life: Lww<DepLife>,
}
```

This duplication means:
1. Wasted memory
2. Possible key/value mismatch bugs (though unlikely)

**Design**
Remove `key` from `DepEdge`:
```rust
pub struct DepEdge {
    pub created: Stamp,
    pub life: Lww<DepLife>,
}
```

Callers that need the key get it from the map iteration:
```rust
for (key, edge) in &state.deps {
    // key is available here
}
```

**Design Notes**
- Wire format needs to handle this - on serialization, include key; on deserialization, reconstruct
- `DepEdge::new` and `DepEdge::join` signatures change (no longer take/return key)
- Check all usages of `edge.key` and ensure key is available from context

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `DepEdge` no longer contains `key` field
- [ ] Wire format correctly serializes/deserializes (key comes from map, not struct)
- [ ] All `edge.key` usages updated to use key from iteration context
- [ ] `DepEdge::new`/`join` updated
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/dep.rs, src/git/wire.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs

title: Add bd binary to nix devshell
description: The nix flake devShell should include the bd binary so hooks like 'bd prime' work inside nix develop. Currently bd is only available after building from source.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Refactor executor.rs to use require_live helpers
closed_reason: Refactored executor.rs to use require_live/require_live_mut with MapLiveError trait. Eliminated all .unwrap() calls on bead lookups.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Allow cycles for Related deps, keep DAG enforcement for Blocks
description:
**Problem**
`apply_add_dep` rejects ANY cycle regardless of dep kind. But "related" edges being acyclic is unnecessarily strict - related beads can absolutely reference each other cyclically.

**Design**
- `Related` and `DiscoveredFrom`: allow cycles (these are informational links)
- `Blocks` and `Parent`: enforce DAG (these have ordering semantics)

**Design Notes**
Open question: what about `DependsOn`? Could argue either way:
- DAG: "cant start A until B done" implies ordering
- Allow cycles: maybe you want mutual dependencies that resolve together?

Suggest: enforce DAG for `DependsOn` initially (safer), can relax later.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd dep add A B --kind=related` succeeds even if B->A exists
- [ ] `bd dep add A B --kind=blocks` still rejects cycles
- [ ] Tests for both cases

**Files:** src/daemon/executor.rs (apply_add_dep), src/core/dep.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Wire parsing does extra allocations
description:
**Problem**
`git/wire.rs` repeatedly does `String::from_utf8(bytes.to_vec())` for blobs. Avoidable copy.

**Design**
Use `std::str::from_utf8(bytes)?` and parse from the slice, or stream JSONL via `serde_json::Deserializer`.

Not a moral issue, just free perf + less churn.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No unnecessary `.to_vec()` in blob parsing
- [ ] Benchmark shows improvement (or at least no regression)

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs

title: Add --parent flag to bd update command
description:
**Problem**
Cannot reparent a bead after creation. Beads created independently cannot be linked to an epic as children later.

Currently `--parent` only works at create time:
```
bd create "subtask" --parent=bd-epic
```

**Solution**
Add `--parent` flag to `bd update`:
```
bd update bd-xyz --parent=bd-epic
```

**Design Notes**
- Should validate parent exists and is not deleted
- Should prevent circular parent relationships
- Consider `--parent=""` or `--no-parent` to unparent a bead
- Parent changes should be LWW like other fields
- ID format: beads created with --parent get hierarchical IDs (bd-abc.1). Reparenting existing beads probably keeps their original ID rather than renumbering.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd update <id> --parent <epic-id>` works
- [ ] Invalid/deleted parent rejected with clear error
- [ ] Circular parent reference rejected
- [ ] Can unparent a bead (remove from epic)
- [ ] `bd show` reflects new parent
- [ ] `bd epic status` includes reparented beads
darin@book
title: darin@book
WAL read/merge failures must not be ignored
description:
**Problem**
`ensure_repo_loaded` ignores WAL read errors and WAL merge errors (`if let Ok(Some(wal_entry))` + warn on `join` failure). Corrupted WAL or merge conflicts can silently drop unsynced data.

**Design**
Treat WAL read/merge failures as fatal for load: return an OpError that surfaces the WAL issue, keep the WAL file intact, and avoid overwriting state with stale git data. Add a test that injects a corrupted WAL and asserts load returns an error.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL read errors are returned to the caller (no silent ignore)
- [ ] WAL merge conflicts return a structured error
- [ ] WAL file is preserved on error
- [ ] Tests cover corrupted WAL and merge failure paths
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/wal.rs, src/daemon/ops.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add LoadedRemote proof type to eliminate repo_state unwraps
closed_reason: Added LoadedRemote proof type. ensure_repo_loaded/fresh return LoadedRemote, repo_state/repo_state_mut take &LoadedRemote and are infallible. All .unwrap() calls on repo_state lookups removed.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Smart commit message threshold (5) is hardcoded
description:
## What's Wrong
The threshold for switching from detailed to count-based commit messages is hardcoded to 5 in to_commit_message().

## Where
- src/git/sync.rs:92 (`if !self.details.is_empty() && total <= 5`)

## Why It Matters
Minor - the magic number 5 isn't configurable or documented. Fine for now, but if users want different behavior they can't change it.

## Suggested Fix
Either document why 5, or make it a const at module level.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add integrity checksum for store blobs
description:
**Problem**
State blobs are trusted JSON; corruption is only detected if JSON parsing fails. Silent corruption could lead to inconsistent state or data loss.

**Design**
Include a checksum (e.g., SHA256) in `meta.json` for state/tombstones/deps blobs and verify on read. On mismatch, surface a clear error and avoid overwriting state.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Checksums written on commit
- [ ] Checksums verified on load
- [ ] Corruption returns a structured error

**Files:** src/git/wire.rs, src/git/sync.rs, src/api/mod.rs, SPEC.md

title: Git fetch should use explicit refspec + surface errors
description:
**Problem**
`remote.fetch(&["refs/heads/beads/store"], ...)` relies on libgit2 refspec defaults; it may only update FETCH_HEAD and leave `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store` stale. Fetch errors are also ignored, so the daemon can sync against stale remote state without any signal.

**Design**
Use an explicit refspec (`refs/heads/beads/store:refs/remotes/origin/beads/store`) and return/propagate fetch errors (or at least surface them in status/IPC). Keep a best-effort path for offline reads but make failures visible.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Fetch updates `refs/remotes/origin/beads/store`
- [ ] Fetch errors are surfaced (status/IPC/logs)
- [ ] Tests cover refspec + fetch failure behavior

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/git_worker.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
init_beads_ref must not overwrite existing local ref
description:
**Problem**
`init_beads_ref` fetches and force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to remote even if a local ref already exists. If local has data, this can overwrite it.

**Design**
If local ref exists, treat init as a no-op (already initialized). Only fast-forward local when it does not exist. Never force-update without preserving local history. Add a test that ensures init does not modify an existing local ref.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] init is a no-op when local ref exists
- [ ] No forced updates on existing local refs
- [ ] Tests cover init with existing local ref
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Scheduler spawns unbounded threads on reschedule (threadpocalypse)
description:
**Problem**
`src/daemon/scheduler.rs` spawns a new thread on every schedule call, and reschedules do not cancel old sleepers. Under bursty ops, this devolves into "death by a thousand sleeping threads".

**Design**
Single timer mechanism:
- Keep a min-heap of `(deadline, remote)`
- State loop computes next deadline and uses `crossbeam::channel::after(next - now)` (or one dedicated timer thread)
- When rescheduling, update the heap entry (or push and ignore stale entries by checking current deadline in a map)

This makes the daemon "boring" which is good.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] At most 1 timer thread (or zero with channel-based approach)
- [ ] Rapid reschedules dont accumulate sleeping threads
- [ ] Stress test: 1000 rapid mutations, verify thread count stays bounded
- [ ] Existing sync behavior preserved (debounce still works)

**Files:** src/daemon/scheduler.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
rustfmt.toml uses unstable options on stable
description:
## What's Wrong
cargo fmt prints warnings because rustfmt.toml sets unstable_features=true and group_imports=StdExternalCrate, which require nightly rustfmt.

## Where
- rustfmt.toml

## Why It Matters
Noisy output in CI/local workflows (for example, just release-patch runs cargo fmt -- --check). It can also cause formatting drift if contributors have different toolchains.

## Options
1. Remove unstable settings and rely on stable rustfmt defaults.
2. Pin nightly rustfmt in dev shell/CI to support the config.

---
Discovered while: running cargo fmt during migration slug work.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Remote URL normalization should canonicalize ssh/https variants
description:
**Problem**
State sharing keys by normalized remote URL, but normalization is currently minimal (trim slash + parse). ssh/https variants (e.g., `git@github.com:org/repo` vs `https://github.com/org/repo`) can create split state.

**Design**
Canonicalize host + path across ssh/https/file URLs. Consider using `git2::Remote::url()` parsing or a dedicated normalizer with test cases.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] ssh/https URLs normalize to same key
- [ ] Test coverage for common URL variants

**Files:** src/daemon/remote.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Persist WAL in durable data dir
description:
**Problem**
WAL files are stored under the daemon socket dir (often XDG_RUNTIME_DIR). Those locations are ephemeral and can be cleared on reboot/cleanup. If sync hasn't completed, a reboot can drop WAL and lose acknowledged mutations.

**Design**
Store WALs in a persistent data directory (XDG_DATA_HOME/beads-rs/wal). Keep socket in runtime dir but decouple WAL path. Add migration from legacy runtime WAL dir to new persistent dir on daemon startup, preserving newer WAL if both exist. Allow env override for tests.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL persists across daemon restarts/reboots (no runtime dir dependency).
- [ ] Legacy runtime WALs are migrated or preserved with a warning.
- [ ] Tests cover migration + legacy format.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal.rs, src/daemon/run.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add with_mutation helper for borrow-checker friendly mutations
description:
**Problem**
Borrow checker forces this awkward pattern:
1. Borrow `repo_state` to check something
2. Need `&mut self` to tick the clock
3. Drop borrow, re-borrow, and `unwrap()` because "I proved it exists earlier"

**Design**
Wrap mutation in a helper that ticks before handing out the borrow:
```rust
impl Daemon {
    fn with_mutation<R>(
        &mut self,
        loaded: &LoadedRemote,  // or repo: &Path + git_tx
        f: impl FnOnce(&mut RepoState, Stamp) -> Result<R, OpError>,
    ) -> Result<R, OpError> {
        let write = self.clock_mut().tick();
        let stamp = Stamp { at: write, by: self.actor().clone() };
        let rs = self.repo_state_mut(loaded);
        f(rs, stamp)
    }
}
```

Now `apply_update`/`apply_close`/`apply_claim` can do "check + mutate" in one borrow.

**Design Notes**
- Depends on `LoadedRemote` existing (bd-xxx) OR can take repo+git_tx and call ensure_repo_loaded internally
- Consider whether stamp should be passed in or returned for callers that need it after
- May want variants: `with_mutation` vs `with_mutation_loaded` depending on whether repo already loaded

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `with_mutation` helper exists in `src/daemon/mod.rs`
- [ ] At least one executor method uses it (start with simplest case like `apply_claim`)
- [ ] Pattern eliminates the "check, drop, tick, re-fetch, unwrap" sequence
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
closed_reason: Added with_mutation helper to Daemon, refactored apply_claim to use it. Pattern eliminates the 'check, drop, tick, re-fetch, unwrap' sequence.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Add serde boundary validation for identity types (BeadId, ActorId, NoteId, Priority)
closed_reason: Added serde boundary validation via try_from/into for BeadId, ActorId, NoteId, and Priority. Invalid values now fail at deserialization rather than scattered through handlers. Added 6 tests for the new validation behavior.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Daemon restart on client version mismatch
description:
## What's Wrong
If an older `bd daemon run` process is already running, a newer `bd` CLI will connect to it and may hit confusing errors when behavior/protocol expectations change (e.g. bead ID parsing rules).

## Where
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: client connects to existing socket without verifying daemon version
- src/daemon/run.rs: daemon is spawned from current executable only when no daemon is reachable

## Why It Matters
Upgrading `bd` can silently keep using an older daemon until the socket disappears. This can break new features (like non-`bd-` id slugs) and makes failures look like user input errors.

## Suggested Fix
Add a lightweight handshake (e.g. Request::Ping returns daemon version/build hash). If mismatch with client, have client request shutdown + respawn, or refuse with a clear message.

---
Discovered while: implementing beads-go migration root slug support.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
CI docs drift: xtask/coverage references but no xtask crate
description:
## What's Wrong
Repo docs/config mention a coverage job and a `cargo xtask coverage` path, but the repo has no `xtask` crate and current workflows don't include the referenced coverage job.

## Where
- .github/AGENTS.md (mentions coverage + xtask)
- .cargo/config.toml (defines `xtask` alias)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (current actual CI)

## Why It Matters
Stale CI documentation misleads contributors and future agents. The `xtask` alias suggests functionality that doesn't exist.

## Suggested Fix
Either:
1) Add an `xtask/` crate and wire a real coverage job, or
2) Remove the alias and update .github/AGENTS.md to match reality.

## Acceptance
- CI docs accurately describe the workflows that actually run.
- No dead `cargo xtask` alias unless backed by a crate.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Fix collision resolution: winner can be deleted by resolution tombstone
description:
**Problem**
In `src/git/collision.rs`, collision resolution remaps the loser and writes a tombstone for the old ID using a "resolution stamp". But the merge rule in `core/state.rs` is: tombstone wins unless the beads `updated_stamp()` is strictly newer.

If the tombstone stamp is newer than the winner beads updated stamp (likely, since resolution stamp is generated "now"), **the winner gets deleted during join**. This violates spec §4.1.1 ("winner retains original id") and is catastrophic.

**Design Options**

*Option A (cleanest):* Lineage-scoped tombstones
Tombstone includes the losing beads `created` stamp (or content-hash) so it only kills that lineage, not the winner occupying the ID.

*Option B (minimal change):* Emit two stamps on collision resolution
- `t_del` for the loser-id tombstone  
- `t_win` for a no-op "bump" on the winner bead so `t_win > t_del`
This makes the winner survive the delete rule while preventing loser resurrection.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Deterministic test: collision where winner is older, loser is newer, resolution happens later
- [ ] Winner survives resolution in all timestamp orderings
- [ ] Loser cannot resurrect after resolution
- [ ] Spec compliance verified

**Files:** src/git/collision.rs, src/core/state.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
